```python
import pytest

def reverse_words(s: str) -> str:
    return ' '.join(s.split()[::-1])

# Happy path tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("hello world", "world hello"),
    ("a b c", "c b a"),
    ("one two three four", "four three two one"),
    ("Python is great", "great is Python"),
])
def test_reverse_words_happy_path(input_str, expected):
    assert reverse_words(input_str) == expected

# Edge case tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_str, expected", [
    ("", ""),  # Empty string
    ("hello", "hello"),  # Single word
    ("   ", ""),  # Only spaces
    ("  hello   world  ", "world hello"),  # Leading/trailing spaces
    ("hello   world", "world hello"),  # Multiple spaces between words
    ("a", "a"),  # Single character
    ("word with, punctuation!", "punctuation! with, word"),  # Punctuation attached to words
    ("single", "single"),  # Another single word
])
def test_reverse_words_edge_cases(input_str, expected):
    assert reverse_words(input_str) == expected

# Error case tests
@pytest.mark.parametrize("input_value", [
    123,  # Integer
    ["hello", "world"],  # List
    {"key": "value"},  # Dictionary
    None,  # None
    3.14,  # Float
])
def test_reverse_words_error_cases(input_value):
    with pytest.raises(AttributeError):
        reverse_words(input_value)
```
